stories.headline: Show HN: Kanban CLI - A Local-First, Agent-First Task Manager
stories.summary: A new open-source tool called Kanban CLI has been introduced as a local-first, agent-first task manager designed specifically for terminal environments. It addresses the growing need for structured control over AI agents in development workflows by emulating issue-tracking and project-management tools.
stories.original_title: [Codeberg.org] Show HN: Kanban CLI (A local-first, agent-first task manager for the terminal) — Hello HN,Ever since agents have become increasingly common in development, I've been scratching my head as to how to control their randomness. Recently, I decided to emulate an issue-tracking and project-management tool for agent-driven workflows.Kanban is a …
stories.context: For years, computer programmers have used a visual method called "Kanban" to organize their work, which simply means moving sticky notes on a board from "to-do" to "done." This new tool brings that same simple, visual organization into the text-based screen where developers type code, allowing them to manage tasks without leaving their main workspace. It is designed to work alongside artificial intelligence assistants, letting users give voice or text commands to update task lists automatically. This reflects a growing trend where software tools are becoming smarter and more integrated, helping humans stay organized while AI handles the heavy lifting of coding.
stories.why_it_matters: This tool helps developers keep track of their AI helpers in one simple place. It stops tasks from getting lost and makes sure the computer programs do what they are supposed to. This could make building software faster and less messy for everyone.
stories.other_side: Some people might find command-line tools too hard to use compared to visual apps. It could also limit who can use it, leaving out non-technical team members who need to see progress.

stories.headline: Top 5 Weather Apps Dominating the Market in 2026
stories.summary: A curated list of the best weather applications available for Android and iPhone in 2026 highlights key features such as accurate forecasts, live radar, and storm alerts. These apps are selected for their clean design and reliability, offering users comprehensive meteorological data directly on their mobile devices.
stories.original_title: [BGR] 5 Of The Best Weather Apps You Can Download In 2026 — Here are the best weather apps you can download in 2026 for Android and iPhone, with accurate forecasts, live radar, storm alerts, and clean designs.
stories.context: Weather forecasting has evolved from simple daily predictions to highly detailed, real-time tracking systems that use satellites and complex computer models to predict storms and temperature changes. In 2026, smartphone apps have become the primary way people access this data, competing fiercely to offer the most accurate and visually clear information to help users plan their days. These top-rated apps stand out because they combine reliable scientific data with user-friendly designs that make it easy for anyone to understand severe weather warnings instantly. The market is dominated by apps that prioritize speed and clarity, ensuring that safety alerts reach people quickly before dangerous weather strikes.
stories.why_it_matters: Good weather apps help people stay safe during storms and plan their daily lives better. As phones get smarter, these apps give us instant alerts that can save lives. This makes our communities more prepared for bad weather events.
stories.other_side: Critics say many apps collect too much personal data just to show you the rain. Some users also complain that free versions are filled with annoying ads that slow down the phone.

stories.headline: WordPress Polyglots Team Seeks Translation Review Support
stories.summary: The team behind the Simply.IN payment gateway plugin has issued a public request to the WordPress Polyglots community for assistance with translation reviews. This follow-up effort aims to resolve ongoing localization issues that have persisted since their initial appeal, ensuring better accessibility for non-English speakers.
stories.original_title: [Wordpress.org] Hi Polyglots team  — We’re the team behind the Simply.IN payment gateway plugin and we’d like to renew our earlier request for help with translation review. Some of you may remember our previous post on the same topic – we wanted to follow up because the situation hasn’t fully re…
stories.context: WordPress is a popular website-building platform used by millions of people around the world, but it is originally written in English, which creates a barrier for non-English speakers. To fix this, a group of volunteers called the "Polyglots Team" works tirelessly to translate the software into different languages so it can be used globally. The team behind the Simply.IN payment plugin is asking for help from this community to check and improve their translations, ensuring that people who speak other languages can use the payment tool safely and correctly. This effort is crucial for making the internet more accessible, as it allows small businesses and individuals worldwide to accept payments without needing to know English.
stories.why_it_matters: This helps millions of non-English speakers use WordPress without confusion. When software is translated well, more people around the world can build websites easily. It makes the internet a more inclusive place for everyone.
stories.other_side: Volunteers doing translations often work for free and may not be professional translators. This can lead to mistakes or awkward phrasing that confuses users instead of helping them.

stories.headline: Meta CEO Zuckerberg Defends Tech Culture in Controversial Podcast Appearance
stories.summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg faced criticism after arguing on Joe Rogan's podcast that the tech industry requires more 'masculine energy,' a statement made against the backdrop of Facebook's early history of ranking women's appearances. The comment sparked significant debate regarding gender dynamics and corporate culture within the technology sector.
stories.original_title:
[Common Dreams] Hands Off Our Bodies, Hands Off Our Future — Earlier this year, in a horrific conversation
 with white supremacist podcaster Joe Rogan, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg, who started Facebook to rank women’s appearances in 2004, argued that the tech world needs more masculine energy.Any serious look at the tech…
stories.context: Mark Zuckerberg, the founder of Meta (the company behind Facebook), recently appeared on a popular podcast to discuss the culture of the technology industry, arguing that it needs more "masculine energy" to succeed. This statement caused a major backlash because it seemed to ignore the significant contributions of women in tech and clashed with past controversies involving how Facebook has treated female employees and users. The debate highlights ongoing tensions within the tech world about gender equality, workplace culture, and whether traditional masculine traits are truly necessary for innovation. Critics argue that such comments reinforce outdated stereotypes, while supporters believe he was trying to emphasize traits like assertiveness and risk-taking.
stories.why_it_matters: This debate shows how big tech companies are struggling with gender equality. It forces leaders to think about how their words affect workplace culture. The conversation could push companies to hire and treat women better in the future.
stories.other_side: Supporters argue that different personality types bring value to any team. They believe focusing on "energy" is about work style, not gender, and that critics are overreacting to a casual comment.

stories.headline: Anthropic AI Model Discovers Over 10,000 Critical Bugs in Open Source Software
stories.summary: Anthropic's latest AI model, Mythos Preview, has identified more than 10,000 critical vulnerabilities across over 1,000 open-source software projects. The discovery was made during a limited access period with select technology firms and research organizations, highlighting the potential of advanced AI in enhancing software security.
stories.original_title: [The Times of India] Anthropic AI model finds over 10,000 critical bugs across open-source software projects — The AI startup said around 50 carefully selected partners, including technology firms and research organisations, were given limited access to the model over recent weeks. Mythos Preview was used to scan more than 1,000 open-source software projects during th…
stories.context: Open-source software refers to computer programs whose code is freely available for anyone to view, modify, and improve, which helps drive innovation but can also hide security flaws known as "bugs." Anthropic, an artificial intelligence company, used its advanced AI model to scan thousands of these public code projects and found over 10,000 critical weaknesses that could allow hackers to break into systems. This discovery shows that AI can now act as a super-powered security guard, spotting errors that human reviewers might miss due to the sheer volume of code available. By identifying these vulnerabilities early, companies can fix them before they are exploited, making the entire digital infrastructure safer for everyone.
stories.why_it_matters: Finding thousands of hidden errors in code makes the internet much safer. It protects your data from hackers who try to break into systems. This proves that AI can help us fix problems before they cause real harm.
stories.other_side: Relying on AI to find bugs might make developers lazy and stop checking code themselves. There is also a risk that the AI could miss some bugs or flag safe code as dangerous by mistake.
